Graziele, you can write a short email to me now.
Okay. I start with the subject: Meeting Monday.
Good subject. Now write the greeting.
Dear Dr. Carter, thank you for the call.
Very polite. Now the message.
I confirm our meeting on Monday at three.
Perfect. Can you add one more line?
Great. Now finish the email.
Best regards, Graziele. I send it now!

Hello Graziele. I am reading you my email. Subject: Meeting Confirmation. Dear Graziele, thank you for your message. I confirm our meeting on Monday at three o'clock. I am available in the afternoon, but not in the morning. Please reply if you have a question. Best regards, Doctor Carter.

Hi Graziele, this is Ana. A short tip about emails. Every email has three parts. First, the greeting: Dear Doctor Carter. Second, the message: write it short and clear. Third, the closing: Best regards, and your name. Start with Dear, and finish with Best regards. It is easy. Good luck with your email!
Graziele writes the subject of her email. She writes the greeting: Dear Dr. Carter. She writes a short message to confirm the meeting. She finishes with Best regards. Then she sends the email.
